hypothesis: If protein sequence design and structure prediction are trained end-to-end in a discrete stochastic autoencoder with a Gumbel-Softmax sequence bottleneck and composite structural losses, then the model will achieve higher sequence recovery and generate designs that refold closer to target backbones (higher TM-score, lower RMSD) than decoupled or single-task baselines because joint optimization learns a shared representation that enforces sequence–structure self-consistency.
